question:
Will the price of Bitcoin be between $96,000 and $98,000 on November 15?

Description: This market will resolve according to the final "Close" price of the Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) on the date specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance BTC/USDT, not according to other sources or spot markets.

evidences.title: Bitcoin's Price History
evidences.content: ### Late 2024 On Nov. 7, 2024, Bitcoin's price hit another all-time high of $75,902 following Donald Trump's re-election, a 9% increase from the closing price on Nov. 5. On Nov. 10, 2024, Bitcoin reached another milestone, crossing the $80,000 threshold.1 On Nov. 11, it continued pushing boundaries, and two days later, it closed at $90,574.1 Market exuberance continued apace in the following weeks, pushing Bitcoin to remarkable highs of $99,817 on Oanda, $99,860 on Coinbase, and $99,852 on Gemini on Nov. 22, 2024, by midday.8 9 10 On Dec. 8, 2024, Bitcoin broke through $100,000 and closed at $101,237.1 [...] On June 13, crypto prices plunged, dropping below $23,000 for the first time since December 2020. By the end of 2022, it was under $20,000.1 Fortunes changed in 2023, as Bitcoin opened the year at $16,688. It rose consistently throughout 2023, ending the year at $43,599.1 ### Important On Dec. 5, 2024, Bitcoin broke through $100,000 on nearly every exchange following news about the appointment of a crypto-friendly SEC Commissioner.3 1 It continuously breaks records following news releases, leading many to wonder what Bitcoin will do next. ## 2024 Price History [...] In 2020,...

It represents what the aggregate market paid for its coins - making it one of the most structurally significant price levels in on-chain analysis. evidences.title: Bitcoin Price: BTC/USD Live Price Chart, Market Cap ...
evidences.content: The halving schedule began with 50 BTC per block in 2009, reducing to 25 BTC (2012), 12.5 BTC (2016), 6.25 BTC (2020), and most recently 3.125 BTC (April 2024). Each halving reduces Bitcoin's inflation rate by half, making it progressively scarcer. Historically, halvings have preceded significant bull markets, with substantial price increases following each event. The 2024 halving occurred in a unique environment with spot ETF approvals and increased institutional adoption. The next halving is projected for 2028, reducing rewards to approximately 1.5625 BTC. By 2140, all bitcoins will be mined, and miners will rely entirely on transaction fees for revenue. ### Who Are the Largest Bitcoin Holders? [...] Taproot Taproot was activated in November 2021, introducing Schnorr signatures that allow multiple signatures to be combined into one. This makes multi-signature transactions indistinguishable from single-signature transactions, significantly improving privacy — Lightning Network channels can no longer be identified on-chain. Combined with the new Tapscript language, Taproot enables more sophisticated smart contracts with lower fees. The upgrade's enhanced scripting capabilities e...

While we strive to ensure the utmost accuracy in representing exchange balances, it is important to note that these figures might not always encapsulate the entirety of an exchange’s reserves, particularly when exchanges refrain from disclosing their official addresses. We urge users to exercise caution and discretion when utilizing these metrics. Glassnode shall not be held responsible for any discrepancies or potential inaccuracies. Please read our Transparency Notice when using exchange data. evidences.title: BIGGEST BTC OPTIONS EXPIRY IS NEXT WEEK 🚨
evidences.content: But options don’t just sit there. Big players hedge these positions using spot and futures… And those hedges create real buying and selling pressure. That’s why price action feels strange into big expiries: • sharp wicks • fake breakouts • choppy ranges that trap both longs and shorts One level matters more than the rest: 96,000. This is the max pain zone — where option buyers lose the most at settlement. BTC doesn’t have to go there, but when $23B+ expires in a single day, price often reacts around these strike levels. Typical behavior before expiry: • messy, sideways action • stop hunts on both sides • breakouts that don’t follow through After expiry: • hedging pressure fades • liquidity clears • price starts moving cleaner again [...] • liquidity clears • price starts moving cleaner again Dec 26 is the reset point. Until then, expect traps, manage risk, and don’t get emotional 🐼 $BTC$SOL Image 4: BTC BTCUSDT Perp 60,102.2 +2.86% #USNonFarmPayrollReport#CPIWatchImage 5#USJobsData#BTCVSGOLD Disclaimer: Includes third-party opinions. No advice. Binance AI may be used without guarantee.See T&Cs. BTC+2.94%SOL+5.69% 11 113 31k 5 Reply View 56 replies Join global crypto users on Bin...

evidences.title: Bitcoin Price History and Historical Data | CoinMarketCap
evidences.content: Bitcoin's price history is a testament to its evolution and growth over the years. It started with a value of almost nothing and has grown to be one of the most valuable assets in the world. The price history shows a pattern of sharp rises followed by corrections, reflecting the volatile nature of the cryptocurrency market. It also shows periods of stability, indicating growing acceptance and use of Bitcoin. The price history is a reflection of Bitcoin's journey, from being a fringe asset to becoming a mainstream financial instrument. ### How Has Bitcoin's Market Cap History Shaped Its Perception in the Financial World? [...] Bitcoin's market cap history has played a significant role in shaping its perception in the financial world. As the market cap has grown, so has the recognition and acceptance of Bitcoin as a legitimate asset class. The increase in market cap has attracted institutional investors, hedge funds, and even governments, which has further boosted its credibility. However, the fluctuating market cap has also highlighted the volatility and risk associated with Bitcoin, leading to debates about its role in the financial system. Despite the controversies, the growing...

evidences.title: Historical Market Data - Binance.US
evidences.content: Data Products Open API ## Frequently Asked Questions Candlestick Also known as k-line, is distinguished by its open time and supports multiple intervals for analysis and interpretation. Download Field Name Description open_time Candlestick open time in Unix time format open Open price high High price low Low price close Close price volume Volume close_time Candlestick close time in Unix time format quote_volume Quote asset volume count Number of trades taker_buy_base_volume Taker buy base asset volume during this period taker_buy_quote_volume Taker buy quote asset volume during this period Trades Executed transactions updated at tick level throughout daily or monthly intervals. Download Field Name Description id Trade ID price Price qty [...] All use of Historical Market Data is subject toBinance.US’ Terms of Use. evidences.title: Treasury Auctions
evidences.content: 1. Home 2. Resources 3. Treasury Services 4. Treasury Auctions # Treasury Auctions The following resources provide detailed operational guidelines for participating in Treasury Auctions and data on historical auctions. Auction Regulations (Off-site) Tentative Auction Schedule (Off-site) lists announcement dates, auctions dates and settlement dates for U.S. Treasury securities. Treasury Auction Announcements and Results Press Releases (Off-site) Record Setting Auction Data (Off-site) Historical Auction Query (Off-site) Historical Investor Class Auction Allotments (Off-site) Top of Page ## Treasury Services Resources Treasury Services Resources Home Collateral Services Frequently Asked Questions [...] + Products & Services Education News + Communications + Email Notifications + Fed360 + Press Releases + Research Studies About + About Federal Reserve Bank Services + Financial Services Leadership Team + Bank Offices + Holiday Schedules + Advisory Groups
